methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cuby Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cover operates in Housing & Construction at the Series B stage, having raised $100M+. The sector's public comp universe is empty, while the private peer set consists of two companies: ICON and Cuby Technologies. This scarcity of direct comparables limits traditional peer-based valuation approaches.

Stage-progression analysis, grounded in the $100M total capital raised as a proxy for post-Series A valuation, is the primary applicable method. Applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to early growth progression yields an implied valuation of $190M. This method assumes typical follow-on round sizing and investor markup patterns but relies on the assumption that total raised accurately reflects post-Series A enterprise value.

Peer-median triangulation was not applicable; the Housing & Construction comp set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum density thresholds (fewer than three datapoints after filtering). Relying on all-sector medians would introduce uncontrolled sector-specific bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, centered on $190M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression estimate. Key uncertainties include the true post-Series A valuation underlying the raised capital figure and the representativeness of historical B-stage step-ups for this subsector. Validation against disclosed Series B round details or revised comp sourcing would strengthen conviction.
